Social Object: Seven in ten creative professionals experienced burnout last year.
Research Axis: 2024 Mentally Healthy Survey (Never Not Creative, 2,000+ respondents across AU/NZ/US/UK): 70% of media, marketing and creative professionals reported burnout in the past 12 months — 17 percentage points higher than general workforce averages. Burnout in creative fields is a systemic condition, not an individual failure.
Motion Graphic: A field of lit candles. One by one, seventy percent of them extinguish — not dramatically, not all at once. Quietly, in sequence. Three candles remain burning. The three hold steady. The animation does not resolve into encouragement. It holds the number.
Post Copy (500 char): Seven in ten creative professionals experienced burnout last year. Not because they lacked discipline. Because the environment extracted more than it returned. The steep functions as the counter-architecture — a practice that requires your presence and returns your essence. You are not the problem. You are the resource. The Steeping Space is designed to restore what the industry extracts.

Social Object: Your brain constructs your identity during rest, not during effort.
Research Axis: PMC / MDPI Biology (2025): the default mode network integrates autobiographical memory, future projection, and self-referential judgment during periods of undirected rest — not during focused task execution. Identity is assembled during pause. Effort is where identity is expressed.
Motion Graphic: A brain scan, simplified to wireframe. During task execution, specific regions light. When the task stops and rest begins, a wider network illuminates — the default mode, connecting across the whole. The wider network holds longer. Identity is built in the space between.
Post Copy (500 char): The brain's identity-construction network activates during rest — not during effort. Your sense of self, your creative continuity, your narrative coherence — these function as assembled during the pause, not the push. The Steeping Space provides the conditions. The 22 minutes is the construction window. Arrive. Let the network do its work.
Lifestage Alignment: Overachievers / Hustle-Culture Recovery / Practitioners Seeking Evidence

Social Object: Creative practice touching the self produces durable change. Talking about the self does not.
Research Axis: Frontiers in Psychology (2025) — arts therapies and neuroscience editorial: creative arts therapies that engage the practitioner in direct expressive acts produce measurably different neural outcomes than cognitive-verbal therapies alone. Aesthetic experience reaches layers of self-experience that language-based approaches cannot access without the body's participation.
Motion Graphic: Two parallel animations: left — a figure sitting across from someone, talking, gesturing, producing language. Right — a figure writing, the words becoming music, the body visible and engaged. At the bottom of both, a brain scan. The right-side scan shows more simultaneous activation. No text judges either path. The data speaks.
Post Copy (500 char): Creative practice that engages the body produces neural outcomes that language alone cannot reach. The Steeping Space functions as an expressive act, not a reflective exercise — tea, music, writing, and presence working simultaneously on layers of self-experience that analysis cannot access without the body's participation. The practice precedes the insight. The insight is the evidence.
Lifestage Alignment: Practitioners in Therapy / Somatic Learners / Evidence-Seeking Professionals
